patterns.name: RSA Private Key
patterns.description: RSA private key in PEM format
patterns.risk: Can impersonate the key owner, decrypt data, sign documents. If used for SSH, grants server access.
patterns.fix: Never commit private keys. Use SSH agent, secret manager, or mount at runtime.

patterns.name: OpenSSH Private Key
patterns.description: OpenSSH private key (Ed25519, ECDSA, or RSA)
patterns.risk: Grants SSH access to any server that has the corresponding public key in authorized_keys.
patterns.fix: Use SSH agent forwarding. Never commit SSH keys to a repository.

patterns.name: EC Private Key
patterns.description: Elliptic Curve private key in PEM format
patterns.risk: Can sign data, authenticate, and decrypt EC-encrypted content.
patterns.fix: Use a secret manager or mount the key at runtime.

patterns.name: DSA Private Key
patterns.description: DSA private key in PEM format (deprecated algorithm)
patterns.risk: Can sign data and authenticate. DSA is deprecated — migrate to Ed25519.
patterns.fix: Remove and rotate. Migrate to Ed25519 keys.

patterns.name: PGP Private Key Block
patterns.risk: Can decrypt PGP-encrypted data and sign messages as the key owner.
patterns.fix: Use GPG agent. Never store private keys in repositories.

patterns.name: Generic Private Key
patterns.description: PKCS#8 private key in PEM format
patterns.risk: Generic private key — risk depends on what it's used for (TLS, signing, auth).
patterns.fix: Use a certificate manager (ACM, Let's Encrypt auto-renewal) or secret vault.

patterns.name: PKCS12/PFX Password
patterns.description: Password for a PKCS12/PFX certificate bundle
patterns.risk: Can unlock the certificate bundle and extract the private key.
patterns.fix: Store the password in a secret manager, reference by environment variable.
